comment.body:
remember that insider’s interest is a steady decline, not a vol squeeze. Simply, because the former one can be controlled on a higher rate. Because what is the goal? - Check the posts I linked… This is why this announcement was scheduled to AH, and this is why Trump needs these billionars around him (once the job is done they will moved away from the gov friendly). 
So the interest is to find liquidity now.

comment.body:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trading_curb
Read it. 
This is what I refer to at the end of my daily post.
It is in effect. Not every environment is good for intraday reversion trading.
We made reversion tradings when there was no black swan risk and left-tail risk, but steady suppressed intraday vol. 
This is not the same regime. Yet. 
(I may sell vol here betting on liquidity hunt... not decided yet)

But isn't it insane how you came here, we longed Feb, shorted the Feb OpEx (despite everybody called supportive feb opex), shorted the market all the way down, while played for intraday reversion tradings... longed into March OpEx, played the bulltrap while built put positions, and now here we are?

We never been fooled by the market :)))

comment.body: Price action and vol interesting to watch here.  We are at the call/put centroid level and according to this morning's post, need elevated vol to break through.  So far after that smooth ride up, with a decreasing RV, we bump our head on it.  Now price bouncing around under the level, causing RV to increase, so let's see if it can get through.

comment.body:
One better to listen to Ray Dalio. 
A brilliant mind in the industry, and not a permabear. No bear/bull duality pls.
Only bcs somebody sees the weaknesses of a system, doesn't make him bear or bull. But makes him possible to spot out weaknesses and strenghts, over- or underpricings. 
He was able to become a billionare. I also learned a lot from his writings. I even have his books. worth reading
